skills.frame-analogize: Build and stress-test an analogy for an already-understood concept. Use for ELI5, plain-language, metaphor, or 'explain this differently' requests; compares candidates and names where the winning analogy breaks.
skills.frame-dialectic: Put a forming claim on trial: steelman both sides, expose the load-bearing assumption, and name a deciding experiment. Use for devil's advocate, strongest case for/against, or stress-testing a thesis.
skills.frame-first-principles: Rebuild a question or decision from irreducible axioms, separating necessity from convention. Use for first-principles reasoning, challenging assumptions, or rebuilding an approach from scratch.
skills.frame-graft: Design by mapping every primitive of a mature donor model onto a new problem, classifying fit, break, and adapt. Use when asked to reason through another system's structure or map X onto Y.
skills.frame-orthogonal: Factor a tangled problem into independent axes and verify that changing one does not move the others. Use to disentangle concerns, test orthogonality, or expose conflated dimensions.
skills.nav-audit: Read-only codebase architecture audit against deep-module principles. Use for health reviews, architectural smells, feature-readiness checks, or thorough domain-by-domain sweeps; reports findings without changing code.
skills.nav-compose: Author or restructure prose as a navigable deep module: lead with the point, group by knowledge, and keep each fact owned once. Use for ADRs, specs, READMEs, design docs, or tightening document structure.
skills.nav-do: Implement a small, decided, behavior-changing code change without creating a plan artifact. Use for quick features or small fixes; not for behavior-preserving refactors or large/spec-ambiguous work.
skills.nav-map: Render or refresh a bilingual repo-level codebase map from load-bearing files and headers. Use for codebase onboarding, architecture maps, module maps, or refreshing docs/codebase-map/index.html.
skills.nav-plan: Turn a feature spec or change intent into a codebase-grounded implementation plan after auditing touched domains and resolving ambiguity. Writes only the final plan artifact with location consent.
skills.nav-refactor: Execute a behavior-preserving structural refactor using verbatim moves, test gates, and real-app verification. Use to extract, split, decompose, or reorganize code without changing behavior.
skills.nav-sync: Refresh load-bearing files' top headers so their first lines accurately describe purpose, details, and dependencies. Use to add, standardize, or repair file headers after code changes.
skills.reflect-catchup: Reconstruct the current state of work from git, diffs, files, plans, and live context. Use for 'where are we?', resuming after a break, or getting oriented; read-only and focused on present state.
skills.reflect-observe: Surface this session's candidate durable learnings, let the user choose, then write only selected observations or skill feedback. Use to record insights or decide what is worth preserving.
skills.reflect-summarize: Produce a complete, neutral, chronological recap of what this session attempted, decided, changed, and committed. Use for full session summaries; read-only and not a selective learning distillation.
skills.relay-digest: Read a relay coordination repo and show unresolved thoughts that mention the current person. Use for relay catch-up, pending reviews, or 'what needs me?'; read-only and explicitly invoked on Codex.
skills.relay-format: Validate and normalize one relay project's thought frontmatter without changing meaning. Use to lint thoughts, fix metadata, or migrate old relay entries to the current format; diff-gated.
skills.relay-launch: Scaffold a relay coordination project and, when needed, its repository roster. Use to create a relay project or coordination workspace; writes project.yml, thoughts/, and decisions/ behind a diff gate.
skills.relay-register: Add a person to a relay roster and assign their project role. Use to register, onboard, enroll, or role-assign a teammate; writes relay.yml/project.yml behind a diff gate.
skills.relay-report: Append a progress or framing thought to a relay coordination repo so a counterpart can review it. Use to post relay updates or alignment notes; only writes in the relay content repo, behind a diff gate.
skills.relay-review: Respond to a relay thought with acknowledgment, agreement, comment, or requested change by appending a linked thought. Use for relay review or approval; writes one diff-gated response.
skills.relay-settle: Distill agreed relay thoughts into the append-only decision ledger and regenerate the active decisions view. Use to settle, crystallize, pin, or log project decisions; never deletes or moves thoughts.
skills.research-critique: Adversarially review a paper or argument: test claims against evidence, locate real sources of gains, and self-attack criticisms. Use for referee reports, paper critiques, or evidence-strength reviews.
skills.research-dissect: Break a paper, RFC, blog post, or design proposal into Gap, Claim, Mechanism, Evidence, and Conclusion. Also verifies cited-as versus actually-says for individual citations.
skills.research-provenance: Audit citations in your own document by tracing load-bearing claims, numbers, and quotes to verified sources, classifying first-hand, second-hand, or orphaned evidence.
skills.research-untangle: Map relationships across multiple papers: lineage, clusters, contradictions, contested ground, and optionally where a claim fits. Use for literature landscapes and related-work positioning.
skills.shape-align: Decide with the user what is In progress, Next, and Future from current decisions and actual work, then maintain a lean plan.md board. Use to align priorities or refresh what comes next.
skills.shape-build: Drive a blueprints plan's In-progress items to verified completion, serially by default, with planning, scoped execution, checks, and board updates. Use to implement or ship the current plan.
skills.shape-dogfood: Use a completed feature like a real user via browser or CLI, record evidence, and identify workflow friction or missing intent coverage. Use when a built feature feels awkward, incomplete, or unsmooth.
skills.shape-elicit: Converge a conceptual decision or root cause by proposing refutable structures and grilling the user until a choice is clear. Use for undecided concepts or logic holes, not visual comparisons.
skills.shape-mockup: Converge a visual, structural, flow, or option decision by generating a real interactive HTML artifact. Use to mock, compare, diagram, visualize, or try variants; never substitutes prose or ASCII.
skills.shape-position: Author and maintain a project's ratified core principle documents from messy, repeated inputs. Use to establish positioning, graduate durable thoughts, or repair accreted canon through gated edits.
skills.shape-reconcile: Compare blueprints thoughts, plans, and mockups with shipped reality, then propose per-file amend, consolidate, graduate, retire, or prune actions. Read-only until each write is approved.
skills.shape-setup: Scaffold a new project to a verified running baseline using an archetype or grounded exemplar, with staged commits and verification. Use to start repos or projects, not to add features.
